title: Manufacturer's Cost of Goods Sold
event: Ortiz Manufacturing began the year with finished goods of $15,000, completed goods costing $103,000 (COGM), and ended with finished goods of $18,000. Compute cost of goods sold.

computationPaths.narration: Same beginning-plus-additions-minus-ending pattern, final stage: finished goods shelf to customer.

memorize.body: COGS = Beginning FG + COGM − Ending FG. COGM plays the role 'purchases' played for a merchandiser.
COGM ≠ COGS. Manufactured is what left the FLOOR; sold is what left the BUILDING.

questions.prompt: Beg FG $15,000, COGM $103,000, end FG $18,000. COGS = ?

distractors.feedback: That's COGM — goods FINISHED. Some of them are still on the shelf: COGS = 100,000.
FG change applied backwards. Ending inventory is SUBTRACTED — those goods didn't sell.
